title: Exciting Crunchy German Tempeh Skillet
description: A pungent, crunchy German-inspired tempeh skillet featuring dark soy sauce and a dehydrate finish for a keto-friendly indulgence.
instructions: 1. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the tempeh slabs and sear each side until golden brown and crunchy, about 3 minutes per side.
3. Sprinkle the tempeh with garlic powder and ginger, then pour in the dark soy sauce and stir to coat.
4. Add the bell pepper and broccoli to the skillet and sauté with the tempeh for 5 minutes until the vegetables are tender yet still crisp.
5. Drizzle a little water if the mixture starts to stick, but keep the dish mostly dry to preserve crunch.
6. Sprinkle the red chili flakes and salt to taste, stirring to combine.
7. Transfer the skillet to the oven preheated at 400°F (200°C) and bake for 8 minutes to finish the crunch and dehydrate the edges.
8. Remove from oven, sprinkle with sesame seeds, and serve hot straight from the skillet.
